key_insight: Learned generation ordering, rather than logits alone, can materially improve multimodal masked diffusion models for both image alignment and multimodal reasoning.
engineering_relevance: A small control module trained with GRPO offers a practical way to steer diffusion-based generators toward better spatial consistency and comprehension without redesigning the base model.
method: Train a learnable control block to predict generation order
Optimize the controller with Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO)
Apply the learned order to text-to-image synthesis and multimodal understanding tasks
evidence_signal: The abstract reports 4.08% relative improvement on GenEval for text-to-image alignment and 4.85% relative improvement on VLMEvalKit for multimodal understanding.
watch_item: The method adds an extra learned control component, so its benefit may depend on stable GRPO training and may increase system complexity at inference or deployment.
paper_title: Reinforcing the Generation Order of Multimodal Masked Diffusion Models
